turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="wingGrad1" x1="120" y1="130" x2="280" y2="260" gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#7fe9ff" stop-opacity="0.85"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#2aa3ff" stop-opacity="0.25"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="wingGrad2" x1="150" y1="260" x2="310" y2="360" gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#b0f5ff" stop-opacity="0.7"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#2aa3ff" stop-opacity="0.15"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="bodyGrad" x1="250" y1="150" x2="250" y2="380" gradientUnits="userSpaceOnUse">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#35d07f"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#0a6c3a"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <filter id="softGlow" x="-30%" y="-30%" width="160%" height="160%">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="2" result="blur"/>
      <feMerge>
        <feMergeNode in="blur"/>
        <feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/>
      </feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <!-- Wings (back) -->
  <g filter="url(#softGlow)">
    <path d="M120 210 C140 140, 220 105, 270 150 C250 160, 220 195, 210 240 C195 310, 165 335, 140 320 C115 305, 105 270, 120 210 Z"
          fill="url(#wingGrad2)" stroke="#7fe9ff" stroke-opacity="0.35" stroke-width="2"/>
    <path d="M295 150 C350 105, 430 140, 445 210 C455 270, 445 305, 420 320 C390 338, 365 310, 350 240 C340 195, 315 160, 295 150 Z"
          fill="url(#wingGrad2)" stroke="#7fe9ff" stroke-opacity="0.35" stroke-width="2"/>

    <!-- Wing veins -->
    <path d="M190 210 C215 190, 245 185, 255 205" fill="none" stroke="#d9fbff" stroke-width="2" stroke-opacity="0.55"/>
    <path d="M325 210 C300 190, 270 185, 260 205" fill="none" stroke="#d9fbff" stroke-width="2" stroke-opacity="0.55"/>

    <!-- Wings (front) -->
    <path d="M145 245 C160 190, 220 165, 265 190 C260 210, 230 230, 215 265 C190 320, 160 340, 140 335 C125 330, 120 300, 145 245 Z"
          fill="url(#wingGrad1)" stroke="#7fe9ff" stroke-opacity="0.42" stroke-width="2"/>
    <path d="M365 245 C390 300, 385 330, 370 335 C350 340, 320 320, 295 265 C280 230, 250 210, 245 190 C290 165, 350 190, 365 245 Z"
          fill="url(#wingGrad1)" stroke="#7fe9ff" stroke-opacity="0.42" stroke-width="2"/>

    <path d="M225 250 C245 240, 255 240, 265 255" fill="none" stroke="#d9fbff" stroke-width="2" stroke-opacity="0.6"/>
    <path d="M285 255 C295 240, 305 240, 325 250" fill="none" stroke="#d9fbff" stroke-width="2" stroke-opacity="0.6"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Body -->
  <g>
    <!-- Abdomen (segmented) -->
    <path d="M250 155 C240 190, 238 220, 240 245 C242 275, 242 300, 235 330 C228 360, 226 378, 236 395"
          fill="none" stroke="url(#bodyGrad)" stroke-width="22" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <path d="M236 395 C245 414, 245 430, 252 444"
          fill="none" stroke="#0b5a33" stroke-width="16" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.9"/>

    <!-- Spine / central highlight -->
    <path d="M250 155 C240 190, 242 225, 245 255 C248 285, 248 315, 242 340 C237 360, 236 382, 240 405 C244 425, 247 435, 252 444"
          fill="none" stroke="#a8ffe0" stroke-opacity="0.35" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>

    <!-- Segment rings (ellipses) -->
    <g opacity="0.95">
      <ellipse cx="242" cy="202" rx="12" ry="9" fill="#11a864"/>
      <ellipse cx="243" cy="235" rx="12" ry="9" fill="#0f9157"/>
      <ellipse cx="242" cy="268" rx="12" ry="9" fill="#11a864"/>
      <ellipse cx="238" cy="302" rx="12" ry="9" fill="#0f9157"/>
      <ellipse cx="235" cy="336" rx="12" ry="9" fill="#11a864"/>
      <ellipse cx="245" cy="370" rx="11" ry="8" fill="#0f9157"/>
    </g>

    <!-- Head -->
    <g>
      <circle cx="255" cy="145" r="20" fill="#0e3f22"/>
      <circle cx="250" cy="142" r="14" fill="#18c978"/>
      <ellipse cx="235" cy="135" rx="7" ry="5" fill="#eaffff" opacity="0.85"/>
      <ellipse cx="270" cy="135" rx="7" ry="5" fill="#eaffff" opacity="0.85"/>
      <!-- Mandibles / snout -->
      <path d="M250 162 C242 170, 232 175, 220 178" fill="none" stroke="#0e3f22" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      <path d="M255 162 C262 170, 272 175, 285 178" fill="none" stroke="#0e3f22" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    </g>

    <!-- Tail tip -->
    <path d="M252 444 C262 446, 276 450, 285 458" fill="none" stroke="#07351e" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Legs (simple) -->
  <g opacity="0.9" stroke="#0b2f1a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none">
    <path d="M252 280 C235 295, 220 305, 205 310"/>
    <path d="M255 295 C230 315, 215 330, 195 340"/>
    <path d="M245 305 C230 320, 220 330, 208 350"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Small glow around dragonfly -->
  <ellipse cx="260" cy="300" rx="170" ry="150" fill="#2aa3ff" opacity="0.05"/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating dragonfly SVG**

I need to draw a dragonfly using shapes without any text. The plan is to create a simple SVG where I include the wings, body, head, eyes, and legs using paths and ellipses. I’ll focus on the structure and make sure it’s visually appealing while keeping the design straightforward. The final output should be in JSON format to fulfill the requirement! Let’s get started on that SVG design!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

message.reasoning:
**Identifying the insect drawing**

So, I’m trying to figure out this drawing. It looks like a stick figure with green and white highlights, possibly resembling a grasshopper. The long legs and segmented body make it pretty interesting, and it has wings that could suggest it's a dragonfly or a butterfly. Hmm, the two large wings look more like a butterfly, but my instincts lean toward dragonfly with its slender body and big wings. I think I'll settle on dragonfly for now!**Generating insect guesses**

I'm brainstorming a list of insect-related guesses, aiming for 20 unique words. To start, my top five include dragonfly, damselfly, insect, butterfly, and mayfly. Next, I’ll add more like fly, mosquito, wasp, bee, grasshopper, locust, moth, beetle, cicada, ant, and termite. I’m also considering including "cockroach," but some like shrimp and lizards don’t quite fit the insect theme. I think I’ll finalize this list now!
